_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn to construct a main question and supporting sub-questions for different purposes.

_source.w: We learn to construct a main question and supporting sub-questions for different purposes.
y: Breaking a big question into sub-questions makes complex inquiry manageable and systematic.
Well-structured questions guide research, investigation and critical analysis effectively.
This skill is fundamental to academic research and professional problem-solving.
eg: We formulate a main research question and develop sub-questions that address different aspects.
We construct questions suited to different purposes — investigation, analysis, evaluation, creation.
We refine our questions to ensure they are focused, answerable and lead to meaningful inquiry.
We evaluate the quality of our questions — are they specific enough? Do they lead somewhere useful?
We use sub-questions to break down a complex topic into researchable components.
We adjust our questions as our understanding develops during an investigation.
